title: E Mail Adress from End of Evangelion
display_title: E Mail Adress from End of Evangelion
wikitext:
[]: E-Mail Adress from End of Evangelion             Quote Post by Director Black » Sun Feb 05, 2017 7:43 pm Scrolling through the wiki, I came across the translations for the quote unquote "death threats" Anno got . But I'm not here to analyze it, but instead come across this: ( [email protected] | [email protected] | [email protected] ). You think this e-mail address could be available? I tried to e-mail it but it got sent back to me. Maybe it work if I was in Japan. But besides all that...what would they say if they found out that her e-mail/letter has become a part of something she loves?

[]: Re: E-Mail Adress from End of Evangelion             Quote Post by pwhodges » Mon Feb 06, 2017 12:59 am I wouldn't expect a geographical restriction on an email. And when one is bounced, the response should contain the reason why. Also, that's three email addresses - did each get the same response? (You can PM me the responses if you'd like help interpreting them).

[]: Re: E-Mail Adress from End of Evangelion             Quote Post by Ray » Mon Feb 06, 2017 1:12 am It's been almost thirty years now since the movie came out. The IP addresses/net Domains have probably expired or were re-purposed.

[]: Re: E-Mail Adress from End of Evangelion             Quote Post by pwhodges » Mon Feb 06, 2017 1:39 am Ray wrote: It's been almost thirty years now since the movie came out.

[]: Re: E-Mail Adress from End of Evangelion             Quote Post by Ray » Mon Feb 06, 2017 4:59 am Are you from the future?

[]: Re: E-Mail Adress from End of Evangelion             Quote Post by Director Black » Mon Feb 06, 2017 7:01 pm This is the mail system at host ------- I'm sorry to have to inform you that your message could not be delivered to one or more recipients. It's attached below. For further assistance, please send mail to postmaster. If you do so, please include this problem report. You can delete your own text from the attached returned message.

[]: Re: E-Mail Adress from End of Evangelion             Quote Post by pwhodges » Mon Feb 06, 2017 7:23 pm I would expect there to be some gobbledegook as well, which contains the details (and you even appear to have removed the name of the server sending response, which is also necessary info); but essentially that's saying the the specific recipient is non-existent. It has to be admitted that some services these days dumb down their error messages, but the necessary info is normally still there somewhere. If the message was instant that can also mean that the domain name in the email address is no longer valid, so your server isn't even trying to send anything.

[]: Re: E-Mail Adress from End of Evangelion             Quote Post by Director Black » Mon Feb 06, 2017 7:58 pm I don't mean to sound bitter by saying this, but you think if you do it, it'll be better?
